data.title: Distributed Systems Architecture
data.description: Google.com utilizes a highly distributed system architecture to manage traffic effectively worldwide using data centers.
data.technical_spec: Designed for load balancing, redundancy, and fault tolerance with distributed databases.

data.title: Monolithic and Microservices Integration
data.description: Blending monolithic systems with emerging microservices to improve scalability and maintainability.
data.technical_spec: Critical functionalities remain monolithic; microservices handle specific tasks.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Advanced global load balancing distributes user queries to optimize performance.
data.technical_spec: Leveraging anycast DNS and regional load balancers for efficient request routing.

data.title: Caching Mechanisms
data.description: Heavy reliance on caching to enhance speed and reduce latency.
data.technical_spec: Utilizing extensive in-memory data stores like Google’s proprietary caching solutions.

data.title: Highly Scalable Database
data.description: Utilizes custom solutions like Spanner for globally distributed, scalable databases.
data.technical_spec: Provides consistency and high availability with multi-site replication.
